theme.title: Object Pronouns in Context
theme.description: B1 practice with direct and indirect object pronouns, contrast, and more natural sentence patterns.

lesson.title: Lesson 6: lo/la vs le
lesson.instructions: Contrast direct and indirect object pronouns in realistic B1-level sentences.

exercises.prompt: I know Daniel well, but I almost never speak to him after work.
exercises.answer: Conozco bien a Daniel, pero casi nunca le hablo despues del trabajo.
exercises.explanation: Use the full noun with 'conozco' and indirect object 'le' with 'hablo'.

exercises.prompt: La vi en la conferencia, pero no le hice ninguna pregunta.
exercises.answer: I saw her at the conference, but I did not ask her any questions.
exercises.explanation: 'La' is direct object and 'le' is indirect object.

exercises.prompt: My neighbors invited us to dinner, but we still have not answered them.
exercises.answer: Nuestros vecinos nos invitaron a cenar, pero todavia no les hemos contestado.
exercises.explanation: 'Nos' receives the invitation; 'les' receives the answer.

exercises.prompt: Lo escuche con atencion, aunque no le crei todo.
exercises.answer: I listened to him carefully, although I did not believe everything he told me.
exercises.answer_alt: I listened to him carefully, although I did not believe all of it.
exercises.explanation: The first pronoun is direct object; the second marks the person affected.

exercises.prompt: The manager called Laura this morning, but he did not explain the problem to her clearly.
exercises.answer: El gerente llamo a Laura esta manana, pero no le explico el problema con claridad.
exercises.explanation: Use 'le' with 'explicar' for the person who receives the explanation.

exercises.prompt: Cuando lo salude, le note la voz cansada.
exercises.answer: When I greeted him, I noticed his tired voice.
exercises.answer_alt: When I greeted him, I noticed that his voice sounded tired.
exercises.explanation: This sentence contrasts a direct object with an indirect object in the same context.

exercises.prompt: I understand your idea, but I do not agree with you completely.
exercises.answer: Entiendo tu idea, pero no estoy completamente de acuerdo contigo.
exercises.explanation: A contrast sentence without forcing an unnatural object pronoun where Spanish prefers another structure.

exercises.prompt: La respeto mucho, por eso siempre le pido consejo.
exercises.answer: I respect her a lot, so I always ask her for advice.
exercises.explanation: 'La' is direct object and 'le' is indirect object.
